summary: Verstappen hunts redemption at Spa after back-to-back high-speed crashes force Red Bull to revert to a conventional rear wing, while Antonelli's FP2 pace signals Mercedes could capitalize on McLaren's misfortune. Norris's 10-place grid penalty transforms the championship calculus, handing Verstappen an unexpected opportunity to gain ground despite his setup struggles. A strong result for Verstappen here could prove decisive in the title fight, especially with McLaren's driver lineup fractured by penalties just as they close in on Red Bull's points lead.
